0.q: CsAlaDC-silenced tea leaves exhibited higher MDA and •OH content than that of the control; overexpression of CsAlaDC led to accumulation of EA which mediated ROS homeostasis.

1.q: CsbZIP42 could interact with CsbZIP3 and CsbZIP6 in regulating these environmental stresses.

3.q: the expression level of CsCBF4 in CsCBF4-silenced tea leaves had a significant decrease of 90.57%, accompanied by a significant reduction in the expression level of CsAlaDC

4.q: These results indicate that CsCBF4 positively regulates the transcription of CsAlaDC by binding to the promoter fragment of CsAlaDC.

5.q: the silencing of the CsCBF4 gene reduced the transcriptional levels of CsAlaDC, thereby inhibiting EA synthesis and decreasing osmotic resistance.

6.q: CsbZIP42 could interact with CsbZIP3 and CsbZIP6 in regulating these environmental stresses.

7.q: CsbZIP42 could interact with CsbZIP3 and CsbZIP6 in regulating these environmental stresses.

8.q: CssSAT6 and CssSAT7 form tight gene clusters, categorizing them into a common subclass... close phylogenetic relationships between CssSAT6 and CssSAT7.

9.q: CssSAT4 and CssSAT5 form tight gene clusters... close phylogenetic relationships between CssSAT4 and CssSAT5 corroborate previous observations.

10.q: Ethylamine (EA)... is produced from alanine in tea plants by alanine decarboxylase (CsAlaDC); the silencing of CsAlaDC impairs EA synthesis
